name: righttyper

metadata.name: righttyper
metadata.summary: A fast runtime type hint assistant for Python code.

documentation:
# RightTyper

RightTyper is a Python tool that generates types for your function
arguments and return values. RightTyper lets your code run at nearly full speed with
almost no memory overhead. As a result, you won't experience slow
downs in your code or large memory consumption while using it,
allowing you to integrate it with your standard tests and development
process. By virtue of its design, and in a significant departure from previous approaches,
RightTyper only captures the most commonly used types,
letting a type checker like `mypy` detect possibly incorrect type mismatches in your code.

You can run RightTyper with arbitrary Python programs and it will generate
types for every function that gets executed. It works great in combination with [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org/):

```bash
python3 -m righttyper -m pytest --continue-on-collection-errors /your/test/dir
```

In addition to generating types, RightTyper has the following features:

* It efficiently computes type annotation "coverage" for a file or directory of files
* It infers shape annotations for NumPy/JAX/PyTorch tensors, compatible with [`jaxtyping`](https://docs.kidger.site/jaxtyping/) and [`beartype`](https://github.com/beartype/beartype) or [`typeguard`](https://typeguard.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).


## Usage

To use RightTyper, simply run your script with `righttyper` instead of `python3`:

```bash
righttyper your_script.py [args...]
```

This will execute `your_script.py` with RightTyper's monitoring
enabled. The type signatures of all functions will be recorded and
output to a file named `righttyper.out`. The file contains, for every
function, the signature, and a diff of the original function with the
annotated version. It also optionally (with the `--infer-shapes` flag)
generates `jaxtyping`-compatible shape
annotations for NumPy/JAX/PyTorch tensors. Below is an example:

```diff
test-hints.py:
==============

def barnacle(x: numpy.ndarray) -> numpy.ndarray: ...

- def barnacle(x):
+ def barnacle(x: numpy.ndarray) -> numpy.ndarray:

# Shape annotations
@beartype
def barnacle(x: Float[numpy.ndarray, "10 dim0"]) -> Float[numpy.ndarray, "dim0"]: ...

def fooq(x: int, y: str) -> bool: ...

- def fooq(x: int, y) -> bool:
+ def fooq(x: int, y: str) -> bool:
?                   +++++
```

Below is the full list of options:

```
Usage: python -m righttyper [OPTIONS] [SCRIPT] [ARGS]...

  RightTyper efficiently generates types for your function arguments and
  return values.

Options:
  --all-files                     Process any files encountered, including in
                                  libraries (except for those specified in
                                  --include-files)
  --include-files TEXT            Include only files matching the given regex
                                  pattern.
  --infer-shapes                  Produce tensor shape annotations (compatible
                                  with jaxtyping).
  --srcdir DIRECTORY              Use this directory as the base for imports.
  --overwrite / --no-overwrite    Overwrite files with type information.
                                  [default: no-overwrite]
  --output-files / --no-output-files
                                  Output annotated files (possibly
                                  overwriting, if specified).  [default: no-
                                  output-files]
  --ignore-annotations            Ignore existing annotations and overwrite
                                  with type information.
  -m, --module                    Run the script as a module.
  --verbose                       Print diagnostic information.
  --generate-stubs                Generate stub files (.pyi).
  --type-coverage-by-directory DIRECTORY
                                  Report per-directory type annotation
                                  coverage for all Python files in a directory
                                  and its children.
  --type-coverage-by-file DIRECTORY
                                  Report per-file type annotation coverage for
                                  all Python files in a directory or its
                                  children.
  --type-coverage-summary DIRECTORY
                                  Report uncovered and partially covered files
                                  and functions when performing type
                                  annotation coverage analysis.
  --version                       Show the version and exit.
  --target-overhead FLOAT         Target overhead, as a percentage (e.g., 5).
  --help                          Show this message and exit.
```

## `righttyper`: high performance

In the below example drawn from the pyperformance benchmark suite,
`monkeytype` runs 40x slower than the original program or when
running with `righttyper` (which runs under 3% slower).

```bash
% python3 bm_mdp          
Time elapsed:  6.106977417017333
% righttyper bm_mdp
Time elapsed:  6.299191833997611
% monkeytype run bm_mdp
Time elapsed:  184.57902495900635
# actual time elapsed was 275 seconds, spent post-processing
```

# `righttyper`: low memory consumption

With `monkeytype`, this program also consumes 5GB of RAM; the original
consumes just 21MB. That's an over **200x** increase in memory
consumption. `monkeytype` also leaves behind a 3GB SQLite file.

By contrast, `righttyper`'s memory consumption is just a small
increment over the original program: it consumes about 24MB, just 15%
more.

_NOTE: this is an alpha release and should not be considered production ready._

## Requirements

- Python 3.12 or higher

## How it works

Monkeytype is slow for several reasons. First, it uses Python's `setprofile` functionality
to track every single function call and return, gathers types for all
arguments and the return value. It checks every element of every argument (an O(N) operation).
Finally, it writes these into a SQLite database for later post-processing.

By contrast, RightTyper dynamically applies and removes type checking. It also only randomly samples arguments, letting
it track argument types with low overhead. RightTyper always checks the very first invocation and exit of every function.
It then performs _adaptive sampling_: RightTyper tracks its overhead of operation.
As long as instrumentation overhead remains below a target (configurable with `--target-overhead`, by default 5\%),
it keeps tracking functions. When necessary to reduce overhead, it reduces its rate of function tracking. Whenever
average overhead remains below the target, it continues function tracking.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_process.correct_indentation_issues
documentation: Return a string corresponding to the file contents, but with indentation issues fixed if needed.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_process.collect_data
documentation: Convert the collected data into the expected format for type_annotations

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_utils.unannotated
documentation:
Returns a set of the unannotated arguments and, if
unannotated, the return value (called "return"), for the
given function.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_tool.register_monitoring_callbacks
documentation: Set up tracking for all enters, calls, exits, and yields.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_tool.reset_monitoring
documentation: Clear all monitoring of events.

documentation: Helper method to update _random_vector and _keys.

documentation: Return a shallow copy of the RandomDict

documentation: Create a RandomDict from an iterable of keys, all mapped to the same value.

documentation: Insert or update a key-value pair

documentation: Delete item by swapping with the last element in the random vector

documentation: Return a random key from this dictionary in O(1) time

methods.name: righttyper.random_dict.RandomDict.random_item
documentation: Return a random key-value pair from this dictionary in O(1) time

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_runtime.sample_from_collection
documentation: Samples from a collection, or from an interator/generator whose state we don't mind changing.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_runtime.find_caller_frame
documentation: Attempts to find the stack frame which from which we were called. A bit brittle!

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_runtime.get_type_name
documentation: Returns a type's name as a string.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_runtime._is_instance
documentation: Like isinstance(), but returns the type matched.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_runtime.get_full_type
documentation:
get_full_type takes a value (an instance) as input and returns a string representing its type.

If the value is a collection, it randomly selects an element (or key-value pair) and determines their types.
If the value is a tuple, it determines the types of all elements in the tuple.

For other types, it returns the name of the type.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_runtime.format_annotation
documentation: Format an annotation (type hint) as a string.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_runtime.format_function_definition
documentation: Format the function definition based on its name, argument names, and type hints.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_runtime._source_relative_to_pkg
documentation: Returns a Python source file's path relative to its package

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper_runtime.source_to_module_fqn
documentation: Returns a source file's fully qualified package name, if possible.

functions.name: righttyper.replace_dicts.get_homebrew_cellar_path
documentation: Returns the Homebrew Cellar path by running `brew --cellar`.

functions.name: righttyper.replace_dicts.get_venv_site_packages
documentation:
Returns the site-packages path for a virtual environment, based on sys.prefix.
This function supports both Linux/macOS and Windows.

functions.name: righttyper.replace_dicts.get_user_site_packages
documentation: Returns the path to the user-specific site-packages directory.

functions.name: righttyper.replace_dicts.is_system_installed_package_file
documentation:
Checks whether a given file belongs to a system-installed package, including
Python's standard library, installed packages in site-packages, platform-specific
directories such as lib-dynload, Homebrew paths, and virtual environments.

Args:
    file_path (str): The absolute path to the file to check.

Returns:
    bool: True if the file belongs to a system-installed package, False otherwise.

functions.name: righttyper.replace_dicts.transform_and_run_script
documentation: Transform and run a Python script using AST transformation.

functions.name: righttyper.replace_dicts.replace_dicts
documentation: Replace dict with RandomDict and apply transformation to modules and scripts.

methods.name: righttyper.replace_dicts.DictTransformer.visit_Module
documentation: Insert import at the top of the module only if necessary.

functions.name: righttyper.unified_transformer._dotted_name_to_nodes
documentation: Creates Attribute/Name to build a module name, dotted or not.

functions.name: righttyper.unified_transformer._nodes_to_dotted_name
documentation: Extracts a module name from CST Attribute/Name nodes.

functions.name: righttyper.unified_transformer._nodes_to_top_level_name
documentation: Extracts the top-level name (e.g., 'foo' in 'foo.bar') from CST Attribute/Name nodes.

functions.name: righttyper.unified_transformer._nodes_to_all_dotted_names
documentation: Extracts the list of all module and parent module names from CST Attribute/Name nodes.

functions.name: righttyper.unified_transformer._get_str_attr
documentation: Looks for a str-valued attribute along the given dot-separated attribute path.

functions.name: righttyper.unified_transformer.types_in_annotation
documentation: Extracts all type names included in a type annotation.

functions.name: righttyper.unified_transformer.used_names
documentation: Extracts the names in a module or class.

functions.name: righttyper.unified_transformer.list_rindex
documentation:
Returns either a negative index for the last occurrence of 'item' on the list,
or 0 if not found.

methods.name: righttyper.unified_transformer.UnifiedTransformer._module_for
documentation: Splits a dot name in its module and qualified name parts.

methods.name: righttyper.unified_transformer.UnifiedTransformer._is_valid
documentation: Returns whether the annotation can be parsed.

methods.name: righttyper.unified_transformer.UnifiedTransformer._rename_types
documentation: Renames types in an annotation based on the module name and on any aliases.

methods.name: righttyper.unified_transformer.UnifiedTransformer._unknown_types
documentation: Yields types among those given that are unknown.

methods.name: righttyper.unified_transformer.UnifiedTransformer._process_parameter
documentation: Processes a parameter, either returning an updated parameter or the original one.

methods.name: righttyper.unified_transformer.UnifiedTransformer.leave_Module
documentation: ConstructImportTransformer logic

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper.enter_function
documentation:
Process the function entry point, perform monitoring related operations,
and manage the profiling of function execution.

Args:
    ignore_annotations: true if we ignore existing annotations
    code : CodeType object

Returns:
    str: Status of monitoring

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper.exit_function_worker
documentation:
Function to gather statistics on a function call and determine
whether it should be excluded from profiling, when the function exits.

- If the function name is in the excluded list, it will disable the monitoring right away.
- Otherwise, it calculates the execution time of the function, adds the type of the return value to a set for that function,
  and then disables the monitoring if appropriate.

Args:
code (CodeType): code object of the function.
instruction_offset (int): position of the current instruction.
return_value (Any): return value of the function.
event_type (int): if this is a PY_RETURN (regular return) or a PY_YIELD (yield)

Returns:
int: indicator whether to continue the monitoring, always returns sys.monitoring.DISABLE in this function.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper.find_functions
documentation: Attempts to map back from a code object to the functions that use it.

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper.restart_sampling
documentation:
This function handles the task of clearing the seen functions.
Called when a timer signal is received.

Args:
    _signum: The signal number
    _frame: The current stack frame

functions.name: righttyper.righttyper.main
documentation:
RightTyper efficiently generates types for your function
arguments and return values.

variables.name: righttyper.righttyper.target_overhead
documentation: default

documentation: tensor shape inference
